title: Tectonic control of the subducting Juan Fernández Ridge on the Andean margin near Valparaiso, Chile
abstract: Near the latitude of Valparaiso, Chile, fundamental changes in the Benioff Zone configuration, volcanic arc activity, and the structure of the continental margin occur opposite the subducting Juan Fernández Ridge. Upper plate tectonics related to subduction of the ridge were studied in the two-degree segment offshore Valparaiso, extending from the shelf edge seaward across the eastern end of Juan Fernández Ridge. The ridge uplifts the upper plate and sediments of the Valparaiso Basin are deformed against its southern flank. In the trench axis, the ridge forms a basement barrier behind which sediments 2.5 km deep have ponded.

location.description: Offshore Valparaiso ~32°–33°S; Juan Fernández Ridge subduction zone (CONDOR experiment)

seismic_lines.name: CONDOR MCS and wide-angle profiles — Valparaiso ~32°S

data.name: CONDOR MCS Reflection Profiles — Valparaiso/Juan Fernández Ridge ~32°–33°S
data.description: MCS reflection profiles from CONDOR experiment (SO101, 1993) offshore Valparaiso; images Juan Fernández Ridge uplift of upper plate, Valparaiso Basin deformation, and 2.5 km sediment ponding in trench against ridge

data.name: CONDOR OBH Wide-Angle Data — Valparaiso ~32°–33°S
data.description: OBH wide-angle refraction and reflection data from CONDOR experiment providing velocity structure of the Juan Fernández Ridge collision zone near Valparaiso

data.name: CONDOR Swath Bathymetry — Valparaiso/Juan Fernández Ridge
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from CONDOR cruise mapping the Juan Fernández Ridge and its tectonic influence near Valparaiso

_data_note: CONDOR experiment (SO101, 1993); earlier than CINCA. Foundational study for Valparaiso/JFR collision zone. Data may be at MGDS.

processing.summary: MCS reflection and OBH wide-angle data from the CONDOR experiment (SO101, 1993) offshore Valparaiso (~32°–33°S) were processed to characterize the tectonic effects of Juan Fernández Ridge subduction. The profiles image ridge-induced upper plate uplift, Valparaiso Basin deformation against the ridge, and 2.5 km sediment accumulation in the trench behind the ridge basement barrier.
workflow: 4. Seismic interpretation and correlation with bathymetry
processing.notes: Foundational CONDOR experiment paper for the Juan Fernández Ridge/Valparaiso region

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. 8 authors confirmed correct (von Huene R., Corvalán J., Flueh E.R., Hinz K., Korstgard J., Ranero C.R., Weinrebe W., CONDOR Scientists). Table 1 (page 3) lists all shipboard scientists for SO101, SO103, and SO104 legs of the CONDOR experiment. vessel updated to include all 3 CONDOR cruise legs: SO101/SO103/SO104. year_acquired=1993 (SO101 = first CONDOR leg). Data at MGDS.
